items.title: Oil prices may fall, but gasoline prices won’t. Look at the trap we’re in.
items.description: The Iran war isn’t causing your energy bill to soar. Blame Wall Street for that.

items.title: Artificial intelligence will drive an unprecedented natural-gas deficit, this investor warns. Here are the stocks to buy before the crunch.
items.description: Chronometer Partners’ Matthew Smith says investors and markets are not prepared for a natural-gas shortage.

items.title: Yes, the AI stock selloff looks terrifying. But it might actually save the bull market.
items.description: The hottest tech trades of 2026 just got slammed — smart investors are buying anyway.

items.title: ‘I have a $2,000 deductible’: I reported a cracked windshield on my Hertz rental car. Can they charge me?
items.description: “I never heard a rock hit the windshield.”

items.title: Having $100,000 invested was once the gateway to building real wealth. For young people today, it’s double that.
items.description: While this investing benchmark is still worth celebrating, young people who want to feel rich in today’s economy need to have a lot more.

items.title: Do I use $300,000 of my $1.2 million retirement savings so my daughter can attend her dream college?
items.description: “Our daughter is a highly ranked competitive softball pitcher with a 3.94 GPA.”

items.title: Bond-market weakness makes a strong investment case for this unloved sector, strategist says
items.description: Michael Darda says the yield on the 10-year Treasury will fall, so he likes beleaguered home builders.

items.title: Oil prices climb to six-week high as hopes of de-escalation in Iran diminish
items.description: West Texas Intermediate and Brent crude’s front-month contracts rose almost 4% on Wednesday after an 11th night of attacks in Iran.

items.title: The Treasury market is on the verge of a worrying milestone not seen since 2007
items.description: There are a host of challenges at work as the ‘long’ 30-year Treasury yield approaches its longest stretch above 5% in 19 years

items.title: AT&T’s stock rises after earnings. Here’s why investors are cheering.
items.description: The telecommunications company beat expectations on subscriber growth, free cash flow and profit.
